featured.name: DeerFlow 2.0
featured.tagline: Open-source SuperAgent harness that orchestrates sub-agents, memory, and sandboxes for complex tasks.
featured.why_radar: ByteDance just dropped the agent framework we've all been waiting for. DeerFlow 2.0 isn't another chat wrapper - it's a proper orchestration system that coordinates multiple agents with persistent memory and isolated execution environments. This feels like the missing infrastructure piece for production agent workflows.

featured.name: Context Hub
featured.tagline: Open source tool that gives coding agents up-to-date API documentation.
featured.why_radar: Andrew Ng's team spotted the obvious problem everyone else ignored - agents trained on stale docs are useless when APIs change daily. Context Hub bridges that gap by feeding live documentation into agent workflows. Simple idea, massive impact for anyone building coding agents.

picks.name: Claude Code Review
picks.tagline: Multi-agent system for automated code security and logic analysis.
picks.why_radar: Anthropic's answer to the flood of AI-generated code that nobody has time to properly review. Uses multiple specialised agents to catch security holes and logic bugs that slip past human reviewers. The multi-agent approach is clever - different perspectives catching different failure modes.

picks.name: DenchClaw
picks.tagline: Local CRM built on top of OpenClaw framework.
picks.why_radar: Another entry in the 'local-first AI tools' category that's heating up. DenchClaw takes the OpenClaw foundation and builds a proper CRM on top. Shows how the open ecosystem is starting to layer proper applications on shared infrastructure.

picks.name: Hebbs
picks.tagline: Single-binary memory engine for AI agents with sub-10ms retrieval.
picks.why_radar: Memory is the unsolved problem in agent architecture, and Hebbs tackles it head-on with a focus on speed. Sub-10ms retrieval times in a single binary deployment could be exactly what production agents need. The simplicity is refreshing in a space full of over-engineered solutions.
